claims.public_statement: GHOST runs a Raspberry Pi and ROS 2 USB-webcam AprilTag measurement pipeline with simultaneous formal IMM and heuristic GHOST-MH outputs.
limitations: Does not by itself establish ground-truth accuracy.
USB webcam exact manufacturer/model remains pending physical inventory.

claims.public_statement: The formal IMM exposes measurement-backed tracking, prediction-only propagation, degraded dropout and reacquisition states during the preserved hardware run.
limitations: Behavior evidence is not endpoint-accuracy validation.

claims.public_statement: The repository contains deterministic software-in-the-loop evidence connecting the formal IMM estimate to relative-standoff guidance, bounded control, actuator lag and follower dynamics.
evidence: GHOST Closed-Loop GNC SIL workflow artifact
limitations: Not PX4, hardware-in-the-loop, vehicle, or flight validation.

claims.public_statement: A predeclared controlled stationary trial measured empirical raw AprilTag position covariance over the fixed 15–75 second window.
limitations: Does not validate tracker accuracy or residual whiteness.

claims.public_statement: GHOST achieved a measured position RMSE of <PENDING> over a six-point physical grid in the documented USB-camera setup.
limitations: Setup-specific result; not general production accuracy.

claims.public_statement: The formal IMM produced lower median endpoint prediction error than GHOST-MH for <PENDING CONDITIONS> with <PENDING CONFIDENCE INTERVALS>.
limitations: Conclusions must remain condition-specific and include sample counts, failures and confidence intervals.

claims.public_statement: The Raspberry Pi sustained <PENDING RATE> with <PENDING CPU/MEMORY/TEMPERATURE> during the documented hardware condition.

limitations: No physical vehicle control, flight test, environmental qualification or production validation exists.
